title: crew workflow configuration (.crew.rc)
description: Schema for a project's .crew.rc — the crew workflow configuration written by /crew:adjust and read at runtime by /crew:run, /crew:pulls, and every dispatched agent. Pair this file with `.crew.rc` ("$schema": "./.crew.schema.json") for editor linting and autocomplete.

$schema.description: Path to this schema, enabling editor linting. Defaults to ./.crew.schema.json (the copy /crew:adjust places at the repo root).

config.description: Every project-specific value the crew loop and its agents act on. `none` (string) marks anything the project genuinely lacks.

format-cmd.description: Formatter command used when healing main, or `none`.

e2e-framework.description: Detected e2e framework (e.g. playwright / cypress / pytest), or `none`.

agent-ready-label.description: The label /crew:run filters open issues on — the queue and kill switch (default agent-ready).

ui-label.description: Optional label that turns on the crew:ui-review visual-fidelity gate for a ticket (run Step 10b, before findings) — the UI is verified against the design MCP; default ui, or none to disable.

instructions-label.description: The label /crew:pro plans from — a rough, milestone-sized instruction ticket it turns into a granular board (default instructions).

planned-label.description: The label /crew:pro's planner files created tickets under; epic parents stay here, work tickets get auto-promoted to agent-ready by the orchestrator (default agent-planned).

epic-label.description: The label /crew:pro's planner applies to each feature's epic parent, grouping its work tickets as native sub-issues; the run loop skips epics (default epic).

review-followup-label.description: The label crew:findings files advisory follow-ups under (default review-followup).

findings-assignee.description: GitHub user crew:findings assigns filed follow-ups to, or `none`.

mr-reviewer.description: GitHub user /crew:run requests as reviewer on each finished MR, or `none`.

pulls-triage-label.description: Label on /crew:pulls' per-run triage tracking issue (default pulls-triage).

pulls-hold-label.description: Hold label /crew:pulls applies on park; removing it releases the park (default waiting-for-human).

board.description: GitHub Projects-v2 project number/URL, or `none` for label-only mode.

priority-field.description: Org-level Priority issue field name the loop ranks by (default Priority), or `none`.

priority-field-id.description: The Priority issue-field node id (IFSS_…) so /crew:run skips re-resolving it, or `none`.

priority-labels.description: Comma-separated priority:* label fallback (e.g. high,medium,low) when no issue field exists, or `none`.

status-todo.description: Board column the loop selects the next ticket from (default TODO).

status-in-progress.description: Board column the loop moves a ticket it is working into (default In progress).

status-in-review.description: Board column a finished MR is parked in (default In review).

status-blocked.description: Needs-human / blocked board column for escalations (default Blocked).

status-done.description: Board column /crew:pulls moves a card to after merge (default Done).

base-branch.description: Branch worktrees fork from and MRs target (e.g. main).

worktree-layout.description: bare-clone (fork off .bare/) or standard (fork off the existing checkout).

start-cmd.description: Command that brings the app stack up, or `none`.

readiness-check.description: Health URL / port / log pattern signalling the stack is up, or `none`.

isolation-scheme.description: Issue-derived port / data-namespace recipe evaluated per ticket, or `none`.

crew-identity.description: Optional dedicated GitHub App bot identity (§4.17). Present → every component acts as the bot; omit the whole object to run as the ambient user.

identity-mode.description: github-app to act as the bot; user (or omit this object) for the ambient login.

installation-id.description: Installation ID for the target org/repos.

private-key-path.description: Path to the App private key (.pem), per machine and outside any repo.

token-helper.description: Path to the installed gh-token.sh helper that mints the installation token.
